seo.title: Building Customer Support with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with LangGraph enables complex, stateful workflows combining RAG, CRM integrations, and tool-calling for ticket management and order lookups. However, cyclical graphs and parallel branches introduce risks like infinite loops, state corruption, and policy violations that can damage customer trust. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response accuracy, PII filtering, tone consistency, and compliance in high-risk customer interactions.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
The typical LangGraph customer support agent orchestrates a stateful workflow with nodes for intent classification, knowledge base retrieval (RAG), CRM lookups, and tool execution. The graph maintains conversation state across cycles, using conditional edges for escalation and human-in-the-loop approvals. Parallel branches handle simultaneous ticket creation and order status checks, merging results into a final response.

Core components include a central State model tracking messages, customer context, and action history. Retrieval nodes query vector stores for KB articles, while tool nodes integrate with CRM APIs like Salesforce or Zendesk. Checkpointers persist state for long-running conversations, but schema evolution and merge conflicts create failure points.

Human-in-the-loop breakpoints pause at escalation triggers, but conditional edges can bypass them. The agent culminates in a response node that synthesizes all gathered context while filtering PII and maintaining brand tone.

risks.name: Cyclic graph execution causing infinite state transitions
risks.description: Unterminated loops in escalation or clarification cycles overwhelm resources and frustrate customers
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors state transition depth and conversation cycles, forcing termination after N iterations

risks.name: State corruption when parallel branches merge conflicting data
risks.description: CRM lookup and RAG retrieval return conflicting customer data, causing incorrect ticket creation
risks.mitigation: Vex validates merged state schema and data consistency before response generation

risks.name: Checkpoint deserialization errors after schema changes
risks.description: New PII fields or CRM data structures break resumed conversations mid-customer interaction
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation ensures backward compatibility during state hydration

risks.name: Human-in-the-loop breakpoints bypassed by conditional edges
risks.description: High-value ticket escalations skip approval when routing logic changes dynamically
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces mandatory breakpoints for escalation triggers regardless of graph routing

implementation.title: Core LangGraph State and Nodes
implementation.description: Define the customer support state schema and core nodes for retrieval, tools, and response generation
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated, Sequence
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver

class CustomerState(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[Sequence[str], 'add']
    customer_id: str
    intent: str
    kb_results: list
    crm_data: dict
    ticket_created: bool
    needs_escalation: bool

# RAG Node
def retrieve_kb(state: CustomerState) -> CustomerState:
    # Vector store query logic
    state['kb_results'] = ['FAQ: Return policy...', 'Article: Order tracking...']
    return state

# CRM Tool Node
def crm_lookup(state: CustomerState) -> CustomerState:
    # Mock CRM API call
    state['crm_data'] = {'order_status': 'shipped', 'last_contact': '2024-01-15'}
    return state

# Response Node
def generate_response(state: CustomerState) -> CustomerState:
    response = f"Based on your order {state['customer_id']}, it's shipped. " \
               f"KB: {state['kb_results'][0]}"
    state['messages'] += (response,)
    return state
